channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Ahluwalia Contracts Bags ₹393 Cr Rajasthan Airport Project
item.description: Ahluwalia Contracts (India) Ltd has secured a ₹393.04 crore contract to build a new Greenfield Airport in Bundi, Kota, Rajasthan. Awarded by the Airport Authority of India, the 18-month project significantly boosts the company's order book and future revenue prospects.

item.title: Hindprakash Industries to Sell Gujarat Unit to Cut Debt
item.description: Hindprakash Industries' Board has greenlit the sale of its undertaking in Gujarat's Saykha Industrial Estate. The unit, which contributed ₹0.64 crore to revenue and had a negative net worth of ₹(3.74) crore as of March 31, 2025, is being divested to boost liquidity and pare debt. Shareholder approval via postal ballot is now a key next step for the transaction.

item.title: Indian Sucrose Seeks Vote on ₹741 Crore Deals, Director Reappointment
item.description: Indian Sucrose Limited has initiated a postal ballot process, seeking shareholder approval for eight related-party transactions totaling ₹741.76 crore for FY2026-27. Shareholders will also vote on the re-appointment of Director Mr. Jaitender Kumar. The outcome is crucial for the company's operational continuity and governance.

item.title: APAR Industries Invests $5M in US Arm, Adds ₹10.76 Cr to Green Energy
item.description: APAR Industries' Board has approved a significant capital infusion, allocating up to USD 5 million to its wholly-owned US subsidiary, APAR USA LLC, to support its business needs. Concurrently, the company will invest an additional ₹10.76 crore in associate Clean Max Rudra Private Limited for new hybrid renewable energy projects. This dual strategic move signals a focus on international market expansion and a deepening commitment to the growing renewable energy sector.

item.title: AGI Greenpac: Promoters Boost Stake to 60.32%, Cementing Control
item.description: AGI Greenpac Limited's promoter group, led by Sumita Somany, acquired 50,000 shares on March 16, 2026. This move raises their total stake to 60.32%, strengthening majority control over the packaging firm.

item.title: VA Tech Wabag Allots 8,087 Shares Under ESOP Plan
item.description: VA Tech Wabag Limited has approved the allotment of 8,087 equity shares under its 'WABAG Centenary Stock Option Scheme 2023'. This action leads to a marginal increase in the company's total outstanding shares and its paid-up equity share capital, which remains reported at ₹12.46 crore. The newly issued shares rank pari passu with existing ones, a common practice for employee incentives.

item.title: CRISIL Affirms Chambal Fertilisers' Strong Ratings, But Cuts Borrowing Limits
item.description: CRISIL has confirmed Chambal Fertilisers' strong credit ratings, including 'AA+/Positive' for long-term debt. The agency also lowered the sanctioned limits for its bank loans and commercial paper. Despite these changes, the company's financial health is seen as solid.

item.title: Kabra Extrusion Technik Names New Director, Plans Nariman Point Office Move
item.description: Kabra Extrusion Technik has appointed Mr. Ganpat Lal Dadhich as an Additional Non-Executive Director, bringing over 32 years of experience. The company also plans to shift its registered office from Thane to the prominent business district of Nariman Point in Mumbai. Board committees have been reconstituted, with changes effective mid-March.

item.title: Adani's ₹2 Trillion Capex Push Faces Funding Hurdles
item.description: Adani Group is planning an aggressive ₹2 trillion annual capital expenditure for greenfield infrastructure over the next five years. This major expansion follows a challenging start to 2026 for most group stocks, though Adani Power has seen gains. While analysts praise Adani's infrastructure expertise, significant questions persist about how the group will fund this massive outlay. Brokerages remain optimistic on select stocks like Adani Ports and Adani Power, but successful capital raising is crucial for investor confidence.

item.title: Mobavenue AI Tech CFO Rathod Shifts to Technology Leadership
item.description: Mobavenue AI Tech Limited announced that Chief Financial Officer Tejas Rathod will step down from his financial role on March 31, 2026. He will continue as a Whole-time Director, shifting focus to the company's technology, product development, and innovation initiatives. This move is part of an internal realignment to bolster the technology function and create a clearer separation between financial management and strategic tech leadership.

item.title: Prestige Estates Upgrade: Strong Sales Mask Margin Squeeze
item.description: Prestige Estates Projects (PEPL) has earned a 'Buy' rating from Geojit, driven by a 122% surge in nine-month FY26 pre-sales to ₹22,327 crore and a raised FY26 guidance exceeding ₹30,000 crore. However, this comes as its Q3 EBITDA margin sharply contracted to 22.5% from 34.3% (for the nine months) and the company trades at a high valuation premium to peers. Investors weigh strong operational performance against current profitability pressures and future growth expectations.

item.title: Solar Stocks Surge: Policy Boost for Waaree & Premier
item.description: Waaree Energies and Premier Energies shares surged, hitting three-month highs on Wednesday, driven by robust trading volumes. Waaree Energies climbed 10% while Premier Energies saw an 8% increase, outpacing the broader market. This rally is underpinned by India's strong policy support for domestic solar manufacturing, including measures like Basic Customs Duty and the Approved List of Models and Manufacturers (ALMM). Both companies are expanding capacities, with Premier Energies undertaking a significant capex plan, while Waaree benefits from a substantial order book and diversified portfolio.

item.title: FDC Appoints Finance Veteran Kishore Saletore as Independent Director
item.description: FDC Limited has appointed CA Kishore Saletore as an Additional Independent Director for a five-year term starting April 1, 2026. Saletore brings over three decades of finance expertise, including significant roles at Tata Group and ITC Ltd., specializing in M&A and international expansion. The company's key board committees have also been re-constituted.

item.title: Dhurandhar 2 buzz lifts PVR Inox shares, advance sales cross ₹200 crore
Sai Capital Closes Insider Trading Window April 1 for FY26 Results
item.description: Sai Capital Limited is temporarily closing its stock trading window for key employees and directors starting April 1, 2026. This will remain closed until the company releases its audited financial results for the fourth quarter and the full fiscal year 2026, following SEBI regulations.

item.title: CCL Products Grants ESOP Shares, No Investor Dilution
item.description: CCL Products (India) Limited has issued 319 equity shares following the exercise of stock options under its 2022 ESOP scheme. These shares were allotted from the company's employee trust to an eligible employee. Crucially, the issuance does not alter the total paid-up share capital or the number of outstanding shares, ensuring no dilution for existing shareholders.

item.title: GTV Engineering Board Eyes Larger CHPPL Stake Via Share Swap
item.description: GTV Engineering Limited's Board will meet on March 23, 2026, to consider a share swap and preferential share issue that could increase its stake in Chirchind Hydro Power Private Limited (CHPPL). The company will also review related party deals and investment limits, signaling potential group-level consolidation.

item.title: IOL Chemicals: Promoter Boosts Stake to 16.20% with ₹4.8 Crore Purchase
item.description: NCVI Enterprises Limited, a promoter group entity, has increased its shareholding in IOL Chemicals and Pharmaceuticals Limited. The entity acquired 7,00,000 equity shares for ₹4.82 crore through an on-market transaction, raising its stake from 15.96% to 16.20%. This move signals continued confidence from the promoter group in the company's future prospects.

item.title: Vibhor Steel Tubes: Director Buys 8,000 Shares, Promoter Stake Inches Up
item.description: Mrs. Pratima Sandhir, Whole Time Director and promoter group member of Vibhor Steel Tubes Limited, has acquired 8,000 equity shares via open market transactions on March 17, 2026. This minor increase in her holding to 3,64,010 shares (1.92% stake) signals continued promoter confidence in the company.

item.title: NSE Sets 0.65% IPO Fee, Challenging Indian Bank Pay
item.description: The National Stock Exchange of India has set a modest 0.65% advisory fee for its upcoming $2.5 billion IPO, projecting a $16.25 million fee pool. This rate is significantly lower than the 1.86% average paid by Indian companies in 2025, reflecting a trend of cost control in quasi-sovereign transactions. Banks, including lead managers like Kotak Mahindra Capital, may accept lower fees for prestige and strategic market positioning, a stark contrast to more lucrative private sector deals. This move signals a potential recalibration of investment banking compensation in India amid a robust IPO market.

item.title: Pitampura Leasing Buys 1,291 Max Heights Shares, Lifts Stake to 4.04%
item.description: Pitampura Leasing & Housing Finance Limited has increased its stake in Max Heights Infrastructure Limited by acquiring 1,291 shares. Its holding now stands at 4.04%, following a disclosure made on March 18, 2026, under SEBI takeover regulations.

item.title: BEML Lands First $60M Overseas Metro Order for Africa
item.description: BEML Limited has secured its first Letter of Intent (LOI) for an overseas metro rolling stock order, valued at approximately USD 60 million, destined for the African region. This significant development marks BEML's entry into the international market for metro rolling stock, diversifying its order book and paving the way for future global expansion. The LOI covers the design, manufacture, supply, testing, and commissioning of stainless-steel standard gauge metro rolling stock.

item.title: Raj Television: M Ravindran Acquires 2.7% Stake Off-Market
item.description: Raj Television Network announced an off-market share transfer where promoter M Ravindran acquired 14,00,000 equity shares, representing 2.6968% of the company, from promoter R Vijayalakshmi. This transaction is an inter-se transfer within the promoter group and is exempt from open offer requirements. Critically, the aggregate holding of the promoter group remains unchanged, indicating no shift in overall control of the company.

item.title: Indian Shares Climb as IT, Defence Lead Amid Geopolitical Jitters
item.description: Indian equity indices staged a significant recovery, extending gains for a third consecutive day. The rally was propelled by short covering, a stabilization in crude oil prices, and selective foreign institutional investor (FII) flows into sectors like IT, Defence, Telecom, and Pharmaceuticals. While the Information Technology sector, buoyed by positive analyst outlooks on AI resilience, led the charge, broader market indices also posted healthy gains. This performance occurred despite persistent geopolitical tensions and a weakening rupee, highlighting underlying sector-specific strengths rather than a broad economic upswing. The market exhibited a clear sectoral rotation, moving away from highly valued FMCG and some IT stocks towards more defensive and growth-oriented sectors.

item.title: GAIL India Swaps U.S. LNG for Early Indian Supply Amid Market Volatility
item.description: GAIL (India) Limited has issued a tender to swap two U.S.-loaded LNG cargoes for April-May loading with two cargoes for delivery to India between April 1-15. This strategic move aims to optimize logistics and manage supply chain risks in a fluctuating global energy market, particularly as India's demand grows. The company's P/E ratio stands at 12.39 with a market cap of approximately ₹96,548.80 crore. The stock was trading around ₹147.78 as of March 18, 2026.

item.title: Hindustan Copper: Analyst Firm Anand Rathi Sees 31% Upside, Targets ₹650
item.description: Hindustan Copper's stock is facing short-term pressure from global events and rising fuel costs, leading analysts to trim forecasts. However, Anand Rathi maintains a 'Buy' rating with a ₹650 target, seeing a strong long-term outlook. This optimism stems from its domestic chemical sourcing, which cuts global supply chain risks, and major production capacity expansions set to boost output beyond FY31, positioning it to meet growing demand for energy transition and electric vehicles.

item.title: Mangalam Drugs Promoter Unpledges 89,000 Shares Amidst Operating Losses
item.description: Mangalam Drugs & Organics Limited promoter Aditya Ramniwas Dhoot has released 89,000 pledged shares, representing 0.56% of the company's total share capital. This occurs as the company faces declining financial performance, ongoing operating losses, and a steady decrease in promoter holding, sending mixed signals to investors.

item.title: JOJO LIMITED Closes Trading Window for FY26 Results, Blocks Insider Trades
item.description: JOJO LIMITED has announced the closure of its trading window for designated persons, including promoters and directors, effective April 1, 2026. This is a standard regulatory step in preparation for the announcement of the financial results for the quarter and year ended March 31, 2026. The window will reopen 48 hours post-results declaration, ensuring compliance with SEBI's insider trading norms.

item.title: Rico Auto Industries Closes Trading Window to Prevent Insider Trading
item.description: Rico Auto Industries will close its trading window for insiders, including promoters and directors, starting April 1, 2026. The window will reopen 48 hours after the company announces its Q4 and full-year FY26 financial results. This is a standard step under SEBI rules to prevent misuse of sensitive company information.

item.title: PVR Inox Gains as 'Dhurandhar' Film Success Aids Recovery
item.description: The "Dhurandhar" franchise's record-breaking run is providing a significant boost to PVR Inox, propelling its stock and box office performance. Analysts anticipate bolstered earnings for Q4FY26, driven by strong advance bookings for "Dhurandhar: The Revenge." However, the company's true recovery hinges on its ongoing strategic overhaul, including screen consolidation and a shift to a lower-CAPEX FOCO model, coupled with substantial debt reduction, which are crucial for sustained financial health and valuation re-rating beyond immediate film successes.

item.title: Shringar House of Mangalsutra Plans Mumbai Investor Meet March 25, 2026
item.description: Shringar House of Mangalsutra Limited will host an investor meeting in Mumbai on March 25, 2026. Management will connect with investors and analysts to review publicly available information, aiming for clearer communication on company operations and strategy.

item.title: U GRO Capital Raises ₹45 Cr via NCDs at 9.50% to Fund MSME Lending
item.description: U GRO Capital Limited has approved the allotment of 45,000 Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs) worth ₹45 crore. These listed, senior, secured debentures carry a 13-month tenure and a 9.50% annual coupon rate, payable monthly. The fundraising aims to bolster the company's capital base and support its MSME lending operations. The debentures are proposed to be listed on BSE.

item.title: Standard Capital Markets Raises ₹100 Cr Promoter Funds for Growth
item.description: Standard Capital Markets (SCML) has received a ₹100 crore capital boost from its Promoter Group, bringing total promoter support to ₹295 crore. This injection aims to strengthen SCML's financial position, improve liquidity, and support future growth opportunities.

item.title: Maruti Infra Explains Two-Day Delay in Director Death Notice
item.description: Maruti Infrastructure Limited has formally explained its delay in reporting the demise of Independent Director Mr. Shrikant N. Jhaveri to the BSE. The company cited an inadvertent oversight and lack of awareness regarding the 24-hour SEBI reporting window, assuring regulators of no deliberate intent and promising stricter adherence to disclosure norms moving forward.

item.title: IEL Limited Closes Trading Window for Insiders Ahead of Q4 FY26 Results
item.description: IEL Limited is closing its trading window for designated persons and their relatives starting April 1, 2026. The window will remain shut until 48 hours after the company releases its Q4 and full fiscal year 2026 financial results, a move designed to ensure fair market practices and prevent insider trading.

item.title: Shekhawati Industries: Promoter Gifts 6.5 Lakh Shares, Stake Falls to 22.92%
item.description: Shekhawati Industries Limited reported a change in promoter holdings. Promoter Mukesh Ramniranjan Ruia gifted 650,000 shares to other members of the promoter group. This transfer reduced the group's total ownership from 24.80% to 22.92%.

item.title: SCI Orders India's First Green Methanol Vessel from MDL
item.description: The Shipping Corporation of India (SCI) has signed a contract with Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Ltd (MDL) for one 3000 DWT Methanol Dual Fuel Platform Supply Vessel (PSV). This marks SCI's first vessel to operate on green methanol, supporting India's National Green Hydrogen Mission and broader decarbonisation goals in the maritime sector.

item.title: Shyamkamal Investments Officer Quits Amid Management Shake-up
item.description: Shyamkamal Investments announced its Company Secretary and Compliance Officer, Ms. Dixita Devang Pathak, has resigned effective March 18, 2026, for personal reasons. This latest departure adds to ongoing management changes at the NBFC, which has also seen its promoters significantly reduce their stake.

item.title: Simplex Castings Secures ₹13.02 Crore Order from ThyssenKrupp Group
item.description: Simplex Castings Limited announced a ₹13.02 crore order from ThyssenKrupp Group for specialized industrial parts like Coke Oven Doors, Mechanical Plungers, and Helical Springs. The project has a 13-month completion timeline, marking expansion into new product lines and boosting its international client base.

item.title: NHPC shares in focus on likely Cabinet discussion on hydro projects
